tex: By writing each number correct to 1 significant figure, work out an estimate for $D$.\\[5em]

Three regular polygons A, B and C meet at a point.\\
The interior angles of the polygons are in the ratio \quad $a : b : c = 3 : 4 : 5$.\\[0.3cm]
Show that polygon C has twice the number of sides as polygon B. \\[5cm]

A company sells items either on a website or in shops.\\
The composite bar chart shows the percentage of sales on the website and in shops for January, February and March.

(a) [leftmargin=1.8cm, label=(\alph*)]

(b) In April, $\dfrac{17}{20}$ of the company$’$s sales were on the website.\\[0.3cm]
  On the grid, draw the bar for April. 
[2em]

(c) In February, the company had sales of \$3.5 million.\\[0.3cm]
  Work out the value of sales **in shops** in February.\\[5em]

  \$  million

(d) In May, the company had sales of \$6 million.\\[0.1cm]
 In June, the company had sales of \$7.5 million.\\[0.3cm]
 Find the percentage increase in sales from May to June.\\[3cm]

 

(e) In 2024, the company had total sales of \$52 million.\\[0.1cm]
 This was an increase of 30\% on the total sales for 2023.\\[0.3cm]
 Work out the total sales in 2023.\\[3cm]

  \$  million

The diagram shows a cylinder with radius $r$\,cm and height 16\,cm.\\
A sphere has radius 3\,cm.\\
The volume of the cylinder is equal to the volume of the sphere.\\[0.5cm]
Find the value of $r$.\\[5cm]

$A$, $B$, $C$, $D$ and $E$ lie on a circle.
$FG$ is a tangent to the circle at $C$.
Angle $BAD = 110^\circ$, angle $ADB = 20^\circ$, and angle $BEC = 45^\circ$.

(a) Find angle $BCD$.
Give a geometrical reason for your answer.

(b) (i)  Find angle $DBC$.

 Find angle $DCG$.
